criteria.name: Code Consistency
levels: No consistent naming, formatting, or patterns — mixed conventions everywhere
Some conventions present but inconsistently applied; style varies across files
Conventions are mostly followed; minor inconsistencies in edge cases
Uniform naming, formatting, and patterns throughout; deviations are rare and justified
Exemplary consistency — code reads as if written by one author; conventions reinforce clarity

criteria.name: DRY Compliance
levels: Widespread copy-paste duplication; identical logic repeated in multiple places
Some shared utilities exist but significant duplication remains
Common patterns are extracted; minor duplication in edge cases
Logic is well-factored with clear single sources of truth; duplication is rare
Zero unnecessary duplication; abstractions are clean, minimal, and well-named

criteria.name: Single Responsibility
levels: Functions/modules handle multiple unrelated concerns; no clear boundaries
Some separation exists but responsibilities frequently bleed across boundaries
Most units have a clear purpose; occasional mixing of concerns
Each unit has one well-defined responsibility; changes are localized
Exemplary separation — every function, module, and class has exactly one reason to change

criteria.name: Dependency Management
levels: Tight coupling everywhere; concrete implementations referenced directly across layers
Some abstraction exists but circular or unnecessary dependencies are common
Dependencies generally flow in one direction; most code depends on abstractions
Clean dependency graph; abstractions are well-defined and implementations are swappable
Textbook dependency inversion — all boundaries use abstractions; easy to test and extend
